8:["$","div",null,{"className":"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10 pb-16","children":[["$","article",null,{"className":"lg:col-span-2","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-stone-600 leading-relaxed mb-10 font-medium pl-4 border-l-4 border-orange-500","children":"From linear switches to tactile bumps — everything you need to find the keyboard that fits your hands and your workflow."}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"the-case-for-mechanical","children":"The Case for Mechanical"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"If you type for a living, your keyboard is your primary tool. Yet most of us use whatever came in the box. It's a bit like a chef settling for a gas station knife."}}],["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Mechanical keyboards offer tactile or audible feedback, more durable switches (rated for 50–100 million keystrokes), and significantly better typing feel. Once you go mechanical, going back feels like typing through wet sand."}}],["$","h2","3",{"id":"understanding-switches","children":"Understanding Switches"}],["$","p","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The switch is the heart of the keyboard. Three main categories:"}}],["$","p","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Linear switches</strong> (e.g., Cherry MX Red, Gateron Yellow) press straight down with no bump or click. Smooth, fast, and quiet. Great for gaming and those who hate noise."}}],["$","p","6",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Tactile switches</strong> (e.g., Cherry MX Brown, Boba U4) have a subtle bump mid-press that tells your fingers the keystroke registered. No audible click. The sweet spot for office typing."}}],["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Clicky switches</strong> (e.g., Cherry MX Blue, Kailh BOX White) add an audible click to the tactile bump. Immensely satisfying — and immensely annoying to everyone around you."}}],["$","h2","8",{"id":"top-picks-for-2025","children":"Top Picks for 2025"}],["$","h3","9",{"id":"best-overall-keychron-q2-pro","children":"Best Overall: Keychron Q2 Pro"}],["$","p","10",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The Q2 Pro is a 65% layout keyboard with wireless connectivity, hot-swappable switches, and a machined aluminum body that feels like it cost twice as much as it did. Available with Gateron G Pro switches in red, brown, or blue."}}],["$","h3","11",{"id":"best-budget-keychron-c3-pro","children":"Best Budget: Keychron C3 Pro"}],["$","p","12",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Under $40, full-size layout, hot-swappable, and a gasket mount that significantly reduces typing noise. An absurd amount of keyboard for the price."}}],["$","h3","13",{"id":"best-compact-hhkb-professional-hybrid","children":"Best Compact: HHKB Professional Hybrid"}],["$","p","14",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The 60% layout with Topre electrostatic capacitive switches. Quieter than most mechanicals, a unique typing feel, and a cult following for good reason. Pricey but sublime."}}],["$","h3","15",{"id":"best-wireless-logitech-mx-keys-s","children":"Best Wireless: Logitech MX Keys S"}],["$","p","16",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Not a traditional mechanical, but uses low-profile switches that feel mechanical enough for most people. Excellent wireless range, rechargeable battery, and works seamlessly across multiple devices."}}],["$","h2","17",{"id":"what-about-noise-at-work","children":"What About Noise at Work?"}],["$","p","18",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"If you're in an open office or taking calls from home, tactile switches with O-ring dampeners (small rubber rings that absorb the bottom-out impact) are your best option. Silent linear switches like the Gateron Silent Red are another solid choice."}}],"$Lb","$Lc","$Ld"]}],"$Le","$Lf","$L10"]}],"$L11"]}]
9:["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js","async":true}]
a:["$","$L12",null,{"children":["$","$13",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@14"}]}]
b:["$","h2","19",{"id":"the-layout-question","children":"The Layout Question"}]
c:["$","ul","20",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Full-size (100%)</strong>: All keys including numpad. Maximum functionality, maximum desk footprint."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>TKL (80%)</strong>: Removes the numpad. A popular balance of function and space."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>65%</strong>: Removes function row and numpad but keeps arrow keys. Compact and efficient."}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>60%</strong>: Arrow keys gone too. Pure minimalism — everything via function layers."}}]]}]
d:["$","p","21",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The right choice depends on whether you use those extra keys regularly. When in doubt, start with TKL."}}]
e:["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-10 pt-8 border-t border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm font-medium text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400","children":"Topic:"}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/blog?category=Gear","className":"text-sm font-semibold px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-orange-50 text-orange-700 hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity","children":"Gear"}],[["$","$L3","keyboards",{"href":"/blog/tag/keyboards","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","keyboards"]}],["$","$L3","mechanical",{"href":"/blog/tag/mechanical","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","mechanical"]}],["$","$L3","typing",{"href":"/blog/tag/typing","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","typing"]}],["$","$L3","peripherals",{"href":"/blog/tag/peripherals","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","peripherals"]}]]]}]
